scenario: Pinnacle Fintech runs a Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot service with 30 job workers that activate 11000 jobs per hour. The team annotates handlers with fetchVariables and wants to add a global list under camunda.client.worker.defaults.fetch-variables to enrich every worker with extra audit fields.
question: How does camunda.client.worker.defaults.fetch-variables interact with the annotation list documented in the 8.8 reference?

options.text: The properties reference documents that defaults.fetch-variables extends the list of variables to fetch declared on the worker annotation across all 8.8 job workers.
The properties reference documents that defaults.fetch-variables replaces the list of variables to fetch declared on the worker annotation across all 8.8 job workers.
The properties reference documents that defaults.fetch-variables intersects the list of variables to fetch declared on the worker annotation across all 8.8 job workers.
The properties reference documents that defaults.fetch-variables is ignored whenever the worker annotation declares a non-empty list of variables to fetch.

a.text: Correct. The 8.8 reference says when fetch-variables is set in defaults it extends the annotation list; when it is set in an override it replaces the list instead.

b.text: Incorrect. Replacement semantics apply to override entries, not to defaults; defaults extend the annotation list of variables to fetch.

c.text: Incorrect. The reference does not describe set intersection; it documents extension of the annotation list when set under camunda.client.worker.defaults.

d.text: Incorrect. The defaults value is honored alongside the annotation; the reference documents extension rather than silent suppression.

explanation: The camunda.client.worker.defaults.fetch-variables property extends the annotation list with extra variable names; the same property under camunda.client.worker.override.<job-type> replaces the list instead.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
